title: 'If he's Singham, I am Pushpa': TMC protests over IPS Ajay Sharma's warning on voter intimidation
content: Trinamool Congress supporters in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas protested against an Election Commission-appointed police observer, Ajay Pal Sharma. They accused him of intimidating party workers ahead of the second phase of polling. Sharma had warned of strict action against those influencing voters, prompting a strong response from TMC candidate Jahangir Khan.

title: Maharashtra IS-inspired attack: 'Suspect had desire to attack Hindus,' CM Fadnavis says
content: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis stated a "lone wolf" stabbing of two security guards in Thane is being investigated as potential self-radicalisation. The accused, who recently returned from the US, allegedly targeted Hindus in the name of jihad. Investigators are exploring wider networks, with the ATS and NIA involved. Recovered notes suggest a desire to join ISIS.

title: Sooryavanshi-Yashasvi vs Arya-Prabhsimran: Who holds edge in battle of openers
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Priyansh Arya, and Prabhsimran Singh have all been in brilliant form this season​, and the contest is being viewed as a battle between two opening pairs.

title: Gujarat civic polls: BJP sweeps all 15 corporations, crosses 50% vote share across the board
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has achieved a significant win across all municipal corporations. This victory occurred in the home regions of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah. The saffron party surpassed the 50% mark in every corporation. These civic elections saw a large voter turnout.

title: China gives Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta deadline to abort $2 billion Manus deal, tells you have two weeks to …
content: China has ordered Meta to reverse its $2.5 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus within weeks, citing national security. Beijing claims authority over the Singapore-based firm's China-linked AI technology, demanding the return of assets and data. This move signals a strong warning to Chinese tech founders against exporting sensitive innovations to geopolitical rivals.

title: 'Tough to take': Delhi co-owner breaks silence after DC all out for 75 vs RCB
content: Josh Hazlewood took four wickets and Bhuvneshwar Kumar picked up three as Delhi were bowled out for just 75.

title: Mamata's big test: Will brand 'Didi' deliver again in Bengal?
content: Mamata Banerjee's 'Didi' brand faces its toughest test in West Bengal's elections, balancing welfare schemes and grassroots appeal against BJP's national push.  Despite corruption allegations and voter fatigue, her maternalist state model and street-fighter persona remain key, as she rallies voters against perceived disenfranchisement and external forces.

title: Video: Fire erupts at Russian refinery after Ukraine drone attack, nearby buildings evacuated
content: A Ukrainian drone strike ignited a massive fire at Russia's Tuapse oil refinery on the Black Sea, prompting evacuations. This major Rosneft facility, crucial for fuel exports, had previously halted operations after an earlier drone attack. Authorities are responding to the incident, with the Kremlin confirming measures are being taken.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 41: Ranveer Singh film eyes 9 year record of 'Baahubali 2'
content: On April 28, 2026, ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ reached a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,779.02 crore, placing it just Rs. 10.54 crore away from beating ‘Baahubali 2’ to become the second highest-grossing Indian film globally. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, the film’s India net stands at Rs. 1,131.83 crore, continuing its pursuit of ‘Pushpa 2’s domestic record. Despite the IPL 2026 season and the release of ‘Bhooth Bangla,’ the espionage thriller remains a box office juggernaut.

title: Over 60 injured as overloaded bus overturns on Purvanchal Expressway in UP
content: A speeding luxury bus overturned on the Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur, injuring over 60 passengers. The bus, reportedly overloaded and driven by a conductor or helper, was traveling from Ludhiana to Azamgarh. Rescue operations were swift, with critically injured passengers transferred to higher medical facilities.

title: What global banks said about Anthropic's Mythos model that it ‘refused’ to release publicly
content: Anthropic's powerful AI model, Claude Mythos, capable of finding software vulnerabilities, has prompted a global financial sector scramble. Banks, regulators, and central bankers are urgently assessing risks and accelerating cybersecurity investments. Project Glasswing offers controlled access for defensive security work, highlighting the evolving threat landscape.

title: Greece: Gunman fires at social security office, court; police hunt 89-year-old
content: An 89-year-old man armed with a shotgun wounded an employee at a social security office in Athens before opening fire at a courthouse, injuring several more people. Police are actively searching for the suspect, with the motive behind the attacks currently unknown.

title: Amaravati to Hyderabad in 70 mins, Pune to Mumbai in 48 mins: Vaishnaw's bullet train roadmap
content: Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw unveiled a roadmap for southern India's transportation, highlighting a proposed bullet train network connecting key cities like Amaravati, Hyderabad, Chennai, Bengaluru, Pune, and Mumbai. This initiative aims to foster economic integration by drastically reducing travel times.

title: AAP vs Delhi HC judge: Kejriwal-Sisodia courtroom showdown explained in 10 points
content: Delhi High Court rejected Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from the excise policy case, citing lack of evidence for bias claims. Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia have now refused to appear before the judge, escalating the legal and political standoff. The court warned against forum shopping and undermining judicial independence.

title: 'There can’t be anarchy': SC hears Sabarimala case, flags need for structure in religious institutions
content: The Supreme Court emphasized that religious institutions must operate under established norms, not anarchy, while hearing cases related to women's entry at places of worship. A nine-judge bench stated that the right to manage a religious institution requires regulation within constitutional limits to prevent discrimination and ensure orderly functioning.

title: Exit poll 2026: Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, Puducherry — When and where to watch
content: West Bengal's final phase of assembly elections concluded today, with all eyes on exit poll projections due after 6:30 pm tomorrow. High voter turnout in the first phase across West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry has heightened anticipation for the results. Vote counting for all states is scheduled for May 4.

title: RCB's Suyash Sharma scripts history, becomes the first spinner ever to ...
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru crushed Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026, highlighted by Suyash Sharma’s historic spell of 20 dot balls, equalling the IPL record. DC collapsed to 75 after a 13/6 powerplay. Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood starred with the ball, while RCB chased 76 in just 6.3 overs, sealing a dominant nine-wicket victory.

title: 'Goli maro, thok do culture': TMC accuses BJP of issuing 'death threats' to Mamata
content: A political storm brewed as TMC accused BJP of issuing death threats against Mamata Banerjee, citing a BJP spokesperson's remarks on a viral video of an election official. The BJP dismissed the allegations, calling them a "victim card." The controversy intensifies amid concerns of post-poll violence, with the EC deploying central forces.

title: Odisha man digs up sister’s skeleton, protests outside bank to claim Rs 19,300
content: A tribal man in Keonjhar exhumed his deceased sister's skeleton and brought it to a rural bank to claim her deposited money. Bank officials, frightened by the sight, alerted the police. After police intervention and explanation, the man, unable to provide a death certificate due to illiteracy, eventually reburied his sister's remains.

title: Is your child dealing with things or running away? Recognising emotional warning signs in teens
content: Teenage years present challenges and parents can distinguish between healthy coping and avoidance by observing behavioural changes in teens. Signs like altered sleep, declining academic performance, substance use or intense anger may indicate underlying issues. Early recognition and open communication are crucial for providing support and seeking professional help when needed.

title: 'First step to join ISIS': How US-returned man's attack on guards sparked terror scare near Mumbai
content: A seemingly routine stabbing of two security guards in Mira Road escalated into a major security probe after disturbing notes indicating a desire to join ISIS were found at the accused's residence. The 31-year-old suspect, who previously lived in the US, allegedly targeted the guards after questioning their religion.

title: Hormuz blockade persists, why India’s Russian oil import is down from highs seen in March
content: India’s imports of Russian crude oil have declined 20 per cent month-on-month in April to 1.57 million barrels per day, easing from the sharp surge recorded in March. The spike in March had been driven by the availability of floating cargoes during the Iran conflict.

title: Vinesh alleges ‘deliberate attempt’ by WFI to block return after 20 months
content: Vinesh Phogat has accused the Wrestling Federation of India of “deliberately” blocking her comeback by not allowing her to register for the National Open Ranking tournament, crucial for Asian Games selection. She said the portal closed early and officials didn’t respond. WFI denied the claims, saying no one is being stopped. Vinesh fears missing the event could hurt her chances of returning to top-level competition.

title: Will upside in gold be capped due to rising oil prices? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Any upside in gold prices is likely to be capped by gains in crude oil prices, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Beyond pills for cholesterol management: 6 natural ingredients, foods your heart needs
content: Youngsters face a silent cholesterol epidemic, with alarming rates among urban and rural youth. Misconceptions about youth and appearance mask the real risks.  Experts urge early lipid screening in the 20s, daily movement and consistent, healthy eating. Evidence-backed natural ingredients can complement lifestyle changes for long-term heart health, shifting focus from delayed repair to early investment.

title: Internet fumes after Manu Bhaker asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: At an NRAI event in Delhi, Manu Bhaker was asked about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, sparking online criticism for shifting focus from her Olympic achievements. Bhaker emphasized mentorship, saying age doesn’t limit talent. Social media, including Joy Bhattacharjya, called the question unfair and cricket-centric. Meanwhile, Sooryavanshi continues to shine, becoming the youngest to 1,000 T20 runs and emerging as a promising future star for India.

title: 0% tariffs, visas, $20 bn investment: How India benefits in trade deal with NZ
content: India and New Zealand have signed a 'once-in-a-generation' trade pact, granting Indian exporters duty-free access to New Zealand. The agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion in five years and includes a $20 billion investment commitment from New Zealand. Both nations are optimistic about the pact's potential to boost economic growth and cooperation across various sectors.

title: Hafiz Saeed's close aide, Sheikh Yousaf Afridi, shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Pakistan
content: Lashkar-e-Taiba operative Sheikh Yousaf Afridi, a close aide of Hafiz Saeed, was shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. Afridi, considered a key figure in LeT's regional structure, was killed in Landi Kotal. The incident follows a recent attack on another LeT leader, Amir Hamza.

title: Blast near railway track in Patiala triggers panic, dismembered body found at scene
content: A low-intensity blast near a railway track in Patiala late Monday night has sparked panic. An unidentified, dismembered body was found nearby, though its connection to the explosion is under investigation. The blast caused minor damage to the freight train line. Forensic teams are examining the scene for evidence.

title: What killed Mumbai family of four? Doctors dismiss watermelon scare; probe shifts to toxins
content: Following tragic family deaths in Bhendi Bazaar, doctors reassured the public that watermelons are safe, emphasizing their high water content and hydrating properties. Experts stated that even stale watermelons are unlikely to be fatal, drawing a distinction from rare incidents involving bitter cucumbers. There is no need for widespread panic regarding this popular summer fruit.

title: 'Nobody else should be allowed': Gavaskar urges BCCI action over fresh IPL concern
content: Sunil Gavaskar criticised long IPL match durations, blaming frequent on-field entry of reserve players and staff for delays. Despite BCCI penalties, games often exceed four hours. He called such scenes “unnecessary” and urged stricter limits on field access. Gavaskar also stressed discipline, citing Richie Benaud, and suggested reducing batter readiness time and enforcing penalties to speed up play.

title: AHSEC Assam results released: Direct link to download class 12 scorecards here
content: Assam Higher Secondary (HS) Result 2026 is out today, April 28, for Class 12 students. Over 3 lakh students can check their scorecards online via official websites, DigiLocker, and the UPOLOBDHA app.  The examinations were held from February 11 to March 16. Students need their roll number or name to access results.

title: 89-Year-Old Gunman On The Run After Mass Shooting In Athens
content: The suspect who was armed with a shotgun wounded an employee at the social security office. By the time police officers arrived at the scene, the gunman was gone.

title: In Phase 2 Of Bengal Polls, BJP Not Leaving Anything To Chance
content: With a mix of urban, semi-urban, and rural constituencies, the Presidency Division reflects Bengal's diverse voter base, making it a key battleground for all major parties.

title: Austrian Man Pleads Guilty To Plotting Attack On Taylor Swift Concert
content: Beran A. was led into the courtroom by masked police personnel at the start of his trial on terror charges in a court in Wiener Neustadt, Austria.

title: "Have Witnesses": Shashi Tharoor Rebuts Kiren Rijiju's "Congress Anti-Women" Claim
content: Rijiju said Tharoor and he both agreed that the latter is not anti-women "maybe because he is highly popular among women."

title: Piracy At Poll Hub: CCTV Staff Arrested For Watching Vijay's Movie In Chennai
content: A person has been arrested for allegedly watching a pirated version of actor Vijay's upcoming film "Jana Nayagan" inside the CCTV control room at the Anna University counting centre in Chennai.

title: From Northeast India To Israel: 250 B'nei Menashe Members Begin New Life
content: The group landed at Ben Gurion Airport under a special government initiative aimed at facilitating their return to their ancestral homeland.

title: Mumbai To Face 10% Water Cut From May 15 Amid Drop In Lake Levels
content: The BMC commissioner said that the civic body decided to impose a 10 per cent water cut from May 15 amid uncertainty over rains this year, with forecasts indicating below-normal monsoon due to the El Nino phenomenon.

title: Bengaluru Congress Leader Assaults Minors With Rod For Questioning Affair
content: The complainant alleged that Naidu was involved in an extra-marital relationship with his wife for the past three-four years.

title: Probe Agency Freezes Anil Ambani Group Assets Worth Rs 3,034 Crore
content: The central agency issued a provisional attachment order under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) to attach these assets.

title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Pact: How Students And Consumers Will Benefit
content: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement: 5,000 temporary employment entry visas will be issued for Indian skilled workers each year.

title: BJP Office Peon To Corporator: Rameshbhai Bhil Wins Big In Gujarat Local Body Polls
content: His transition from serving tea and managing files for party leaders to becoming an elected Corporator has become a symbol of grassroots political mobility in North Gujarat.

title: Rare Fish Species 'Gitchak Nakana' Discovered In Assam's Goalpara
content: Researchers were able to study the species after locals pumped out water from the well, bringing the hidden aquatic life to the surface.

title: 'US Defenseless Against Advanced Missiles': Pentagon's Big Admission Amid Iran War
content: The stark assessment comes amid concerns over evolving military capabilities of nations like China, Russia, Iran, and North Korea, forcing Washington to acknowledge major gaps in its homeland missile defence.

title: 34-Year-Old Woman's Naked Body Found In A Pool Of Blood In Bengaluru Flat
content: The incident came to light on Monday after neighbours alerted authorities about a persistent foul smell coming from the flat.

title: If he's Singham, I am Pushpa: TMC leader after U.P.-cadre IPS visits his house, faces protest
content: “Senior TMC leaders and Ministers alleged that IPS officer was overstepping his role. 2011-batch IPS officer faced protests from TMC supporters, who raised 'Jai Bangla' in front of the residence as well as the election office of local TMC candidate Jahangir Khan,” says officials

title: Rohit Pawar calls for CID probe; alleges link between godman Ashok Kharat and Ajit Pawar plane crash
content: Rohit Pawar alleged that some occult rituals had been performed outside late Ajit Pawar's house through a small political leader in Baramati

title: Twist in gold theft at Sattur DMK candidate’s house: estranged daughter-in-law, accomplices arrested
content: While Mr. Kadarkarai Raj was busy campaigning for the elections, unidentified intruders barged into his residence at Venkatachalapuram on the night of April 13 and decamped with 122 sovereigns of gold, cash, and silver articles

title: Lakshmi Parthasarathy orders power, water at Amaravati workers’ camp after inspection
content: ADC Chairperson D. Lakshmi Parthasarathy directed contractors to ensure better food and accommodation for migrant workers at Amaravati construction sites, after she found gaps at a labour camp near E-4 Road

title: ED attaches assets worth ₹3,034.90 crore in RCom case
content: The SIT constituted on the Supreme Court’s directions is probing the cases of alleged diversion and laundering of funds

title: Gujarat local polls: BJP bags nine municipal corporations; counting underway in six others
content: Counting of votes began at 9 a.m. on Tuesday (April 28, 2026), State Election Commission (SEC) officials said

title: 9,400 accounts banned after dedicated probe found links to digital arrests, WhatsApp document in Supreme Court says
content: WhatsApp said its probe fan out from the initial ‘seed’ input from government sources to identify and run down the creators or administrators behind scam operations like digital arrests or accounts actively promoting scam activity within groups and channels, etc

title: Delhi High Court grants one-week interim bail to Engineer Rashid to visit ailing father
content: A Bench of Justices Prathiba M. Singh and Madhu Jain clarified that during this period, Rashid could either visit the hospital where his father is being treated or remain at home

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Wayanad gears up for vote counting on May 4
content: While UDF exudes confidence of a clean sweep, LDF believes it can improve its tally in the hill district. NDA hopes to expand its presence

title: Air India, IndiGo, SpiceJet seek ATF price relief; say airline industry on verge of ‘stopping operations’
content: The West Asia turmoil has pushed up oil prices, and airspace restrictions have increased airlines' operating costs, especially on long-haul routes

title: ‘Kadapa NTR’ Paladugu Rama Naidu, lifelong TDP loyalist, dies at 67
content: Paladugu Rama Naidu, the Kondayapalle-based TDP loyalist who dressed up as N.T. Rama Rao at party meetings and rode a bicycle, the party’s election symbol, through Kadapa rallies, dies at 67

title: Kerala SSLC results on May 15, Plus Two and VHSE on May 22: Minister Sivankutty
content: Minister says more than 2,000 teachers had not participated in the evaluation process, and strict action has been ordered against them

title: High Court fumes at 'audacity' of officials to disobey court orders, issues contempt notice to SSC director
content: The Bench issued the contempt notice to Staff Selection Commission Director R.G. Singh and posted the matter for further hearing on April 29, 2026

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: Amaravati-Hyderabad bullet train to cut travel to 70 minutes; Vaishnaw unveils south India rail plan
content: The South Coast Railway zone, a long-standing demand from Andhra Pradesh, will be notified on June 1, 2026, Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said.

title: Chennai under tight security as 23,000 police, CAPF deployed for Assembly polls
content: A senior police officer said, “Following instructions of Commissioner of Police, Abhin Dinesh Modak, over 21,000 personnel from the Greater Chennai City Police and 2,000 Home Guards have been deployed for bandobust duties from Wednesday

title: Congress reiterates demand for all-party meet on women’s quota implementation
content: Jairam Ramesh said that with the election campaign now over, the Prime Minister should act on the Opposition’s demand — raised consistently since mid-March — for consultations on operationalising the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, 2023

title: 736 polling booths across The Nilgiris gear up for election day
content: Police presence stepped up across The Nilgiris, with checking intensified at major public spaces across the district; a total of 74 vulnerable booths have also been identified

title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
content: 59-year-old accused Salim Dola was handed over to the Narcotics Control Bureau and taken to its headquarters in Delhi

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026 | Reserved constituencies: procedural success, and yet a political struggle
content: Though the primary goal of proportional representation was achieved, a closer perusal of the intention reveals a significant gap between numerical presence and effective empowerment, say experts

title: Assembly election 2026 LIVE: Tribunal clears 1,468 electors to vote in second phase of polls in West Bengal, says official
content: According to the Election Commission, voters whose names appear in the supplementary roll can check details by entering their EPIC number on its website

title: Maruti Suzuki Q4 net profit falls 6.45% to ₹3,659 crore
content: The auto major had posted a net profit of ₹3,911.1 crore in the same quarter of the preceding fiscal, Maruti Suzuki India said in a regulatory filing

title: Google announces water, skilling, fishing-community programmes around Vizag AI hub
content: Alongside the data centre, the company has tied up with five partners on watershed management, GPS for fisherfolk, women’s livelihoods and skills training, with the stated aim of preparing local talent for the hub

title: BJP’s rapid rise in Assam is more pronounced in ST seats
content: The 2026 results will reveal if the BJP will retain its ST-seat stronghold or concede ground to the Congress that is trying to bounce back

title: Six foreigners held for prostitution near Madiwala check post in Bengaluru
content: Upon questioning, the women reportedly failed to produce valid passports or visa documents, or provide satisfactory details regarding their stay in India

title: Bengal elections second phase: Mamata, her Cabinet colleagues face contest in Kolkata and its suburbs
content: Of the remaining 142 seats that are going to polls in the second phase, 111 seats are located in the Presidency region of the State; the Trinamool Congress had won 96 seats and the BJP 14 seats in the 2021 Assembly polls; Kolkata and the adjoining areas have voted for the Trinamool Congress across several elections in the past, but this time the candidates of the ruling party are facing tough contests from the candidates of BJP and Left Front

title: Man killed in suspected elephant attack in Kerala’s Malappuram
content: According to local residents, he was found seriously injured in a forest-fringe area on Tuesday morning and was rushed to a private hospital in Perinthalmanna, where he later succumbed to his injuries

title: Karnataka Public School allegedly made four students write SSLC exams as private candidates in Chikkamagaluru to improve pass percentage
content: The government school administration made the students appear for the exams as private candidates, allegedly without informing the parents, to achieve a high pass percentage

title: Madhya Pradesh Government forms six-member panel under retired Supreme Court Judge for UCC draft bill
content: Madhya Pradesh Government announced that retired SC Judge Justice Ranjana Prasad Desai will head the panel comprising a retired IAS officer, a legal expert, and an academician, among others

title: Five-bed heatstroke ward opened at Erode government hospital
content: The ward will cater to patients suffering from symptoms such as fainting and giddiness; Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) is also being provided

title: Campaign bells fall silent for second phase of WB polls, polling for 142 seats on April 29
content: The 142 seats are spread across seven districts of South Bengal including Kolkata. About 3.21 crore electors will determine fate of 1448 candidates

title: Inspector felicitated by rowdy sheeter at police station in Bengaluru, probe ordered after video goes viral
content: The development comes close on the heels of a protest by a Kannada organisation demanding action against an inspector in Devanahalli who has been accused of accepting bribes and interfering in civil disputes

title: Speak Marathi to stay in driver’s seat: Maharashtra Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik
content: Maharashtra Transport Minister Pratap Sarnaik stated that if one wishes to conduct business in Maharashtra, a working knowledge of Marathi is necessary

title: Rajnath Singh calls for unified SCO action against terrorism, warns against double standards
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said that terrorism cannot be justified under any circumstances, adding that it has no nationality and no theology

title: Kerala BDS student death: Hartal by Dalit, Adivasi groups evokes partial response in Ernakulam
content: Normal life was largely unaffected in Kochi, with shops and government offices functioning as usual, while police intervened to prevent disruptions to transport. Minor clashes were reported in some areas as tensions flared between protesters and the police

title: Cooker bomb blast: BJP accuses Congress of indulging in 'appeasement politics' in Karnataka
content: Leader of Opposition in the Assembly R. Ashok targeted Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar and other Congress leaders in Karnataka, alleging that they had earlier downplayed the incident for political reasons

title: West Bengal polls phase II: BJP veterans and CPI(M) youth brigade put up a challenge in Trinamool stronghold
content: BJP veterans such as Suvendu Adhikari, Swapan Dasgupta, Arjun Singh, Tapas Roy and Left’s fresh faces like Kalatan Dasgupta, Minakshi Mukherjee and Dipsita Dhar take on TMC candidates

title: High Court slams Bengal government for delay in handing over land to BSF for fencing India-Bangladesh border
content: It expressed displeasure over the State government handing over just eight kilometres of land instead of the 127 km as ordered by the court

title: Odisha mining project protest: Congress seeks independent probe; alleges PESA, FRA violations
content: The Congress and Left parties have condemned the Odisha government and alleged that the PESA and Forest acts are being violated in the area to help a private company

title: Troubled waters: A.P., T.N. fishermen embroiled in a row
content: The seizure of four mechanised boats owned by Tamil Nadu fishermen at Juvvaladinne Fishing Harbour in Nellore district by local fisherfolk has prompted the Andhra Pradesh government to reinitiate boat patrolling across four coastal districts, bringing to the fore yet again a four-decade-old issue of illegal intrusion. The conflict takes a political turn and the repercussions are witnessed in 164 A.P. villages, narrates N. S. Chowdary

title: West Bengal polls: Modi and Mamata spar over jhaal muri
content: After voting for the first phase of Assembly polls in West Bengal, the campaign has picked up for the final phase on April 29

title: Caught in unrest after Zubeen Garg’s death, Assam’s Tezpur university starts afresh
content: The central Tezpur University is offering a bunch of new courses and is in the process of acquiring land for two expansive campuses

title: Relief as West Bengal sees bonhomie amid bitter political rivalry
content: Trinamool and BJP candidates at Kharagpur Sadar shake hands, while BJP candidate in Taldangra constituency touches the feet of his rival Trinamool nominee

title: Chandrababu Naidu lays foundation for Google’s ₹1.35 lakh crore AI data centre in Visakhapatnam
content: The 1-Gigawatt hyperscale AI data centre, coming up on 601.4 acres at Tarluvada at an investment of ₹1.35 lakh crore, is one of the largest single FDI projects in India and forms part of Google’s $15 billion commitment to Andhra Pradesh

title: Cabinet reshuffle should take place and everyone should get opportunity, says Karnataka Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao
content: Karnataka Health Minister Dinesh Gundu Rao has said that there are several seniors and experienced leaders in the Congress and they should also get opportunity in the Cabinet reshuffle

The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: Last supper: No chicken for dinner, Telangana man shouts at wife, she kills him with sickle
content: the victim was identified as Kodandam Shivaji from Gosangi Colony . the 28-year-old scrap vendor was attacked with a sickle after he shouted at his wife for not cooking chicken . he collapsed after the assault and sustained a head injury .

title: Humans may soon live and work on the Moon in groundbreaking 2030 plan
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
